more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Bad news for residents despite police releasing photos of Harare’s most wanted armed robbers
more.description: Harare – A palpable sense of fear continues to grip the residents of Harare, particularly in the suburbs of Mabelreign, Marlborough, and Westgate, despite concerted efforts by the Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) to apprehend a notorious gang of armed robbers. The release of clear photographs of suspected culprits, far from bringing reassurance, has instead highlighted [...] The post Bad news for residents despite police releasing photos of Harare’s most wanted armed robbers first appeared on My Zimbabwe News .

more.title: Man arrested for stabbing wife and pregnant daughter to death
more.description: Police have arrested a 45-year-old man in connection with the murder of his wife and pregnant daughter following a domestic dispute in Victory Park, Kadoma. In a statement, Zimbabwe Republic Police (ZRP) spokesperson Paul Nyathi confirmed the arrest of Obert Namurokosi, saying the incident occurred on May 3, 2026. “The ZRP confirms the arrest of [...] The post Man arrested for stabbing wife and pregnant daughter to death appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com .

more.title: Ramaphosa’s recent visit enough evidence that the ANC supports Mnangagwa’s dictatorship – says South Africa’s DA
more.description: A RECENT visit to President Emmerson Mnangagwa’s Precabe Farm by South African President Cyril Ramaphosa is enough evidence that the ANC supports his dictatorship in Zimbabwe, opposition party, Democratic Alliance (DA) has said. more.title: New ZiG notes boost daily transactions
more.description: Source: New ZiG notes boost daily transactions – herald Herald Reporter NEW Zimbabwe Gold banknotes are easing cash shortages and improving daily transactions, with economists saying long-term confidence will depend on the currency’s ability to retain value. more.description: Source: Zimbabwe exports US$11,7 million worth of goods to Europe in a month – NewZimbabwe.com ZIMBABWE exported goods worth US$11,7 million to the European Union (EU) in the month of March 2026 in a development which further demystifies the existence of economic sanctions against the African nation. more.title: Something is brewing in Zimbabwe
